sourceTitle: How to build a help center in Notion

questions.question: What is the first step recommended for creating a Help Center in Notion?

options.text: Start by writing all the help articles from scratch.
options.explanation: While you can write articles from scratch, the guide suggests beginning with a template to speed up setup.

options.text: Browse the Template gallery and select the Help Center template.
options.explanation: The guide advises using Notion’s Help Center template as the starting point.

options.text: Publish the page to the web before adding any content.
options.explanation: Publishing should happen after the content is organized and ready.

options.text: Create a new workspace specifically for the Help Center.
options.explanation: A separate workspace isn’t required; you can build the Help Center within an existing workspace.

questions.question: Which feature allows you to keep content synchronized across multiple pages?

options.text: Embed block
options.explanation: Embed blocks display external content but don’t automatically sync Notion content.

options.text: Paste and sync feature
options.explanation: The guide mentions using the Paste and sync feature to maintain up‑to‑date content across pages.

options.text: Link to page
options.explanation: Linking points to a page but does not sync changes made elsewhere.

options.text: Database view
options.explanation: Database views organize data but aren’t described as a sync mechanism for static pages.

questions.question: How can you make the Help Center’s public URL simpler?

options.text: Rename the page title to a short word.
options.explanation: Changing the title doesn’t affect the public URL.

options.text: Set the page as the Public home page under Settings.
options.explanation: The guide says you may simplify the URL by making the page the Public home page.

options.text: Add a custom domain through Notion’s integrations.
options.explanation: Custom domains are not covered in the article.

options.text: Use a URL shortener service.
options.explanation: While possible, the article does not mention this as a built‑in method.

questions.question: Which visual elements are recommended to align the Help Center with a company’s brand?

options.text: Only the page title font.
options.explanation: Brand alignment involves more than just the title font.

options.text: Cover images and page icons.
options.explanation: The guide suggests adding cover images and choosing icons that match the brand.

options.text: Embedding a company logo in a callout block.
options.explanation: While possible, the article specifically mentions cover images and icons, not embedding logos in callouts.

options.text: Changing the background color of the entire workspace.
options.explanation: Workspace background changes aren’t discussed as a branding method.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using callout blocks and background colors in the Help Center pages?

options.text: To hide important information from users.
options.explanation: Callouts and colors are meant to highlight, not hide, information.

options.text: To improve clarity and visual hierarchy.
options.explanation: The article notes that callout blocks and background colors help with clarity.

options.text: To reduce page load speed.
options.explanation: Visual styling does not affect load speed in Notion.

options.text: To automatically generate SEO meta tags.
options.explanation: SEO meta tags are not created by callout blocks or background colors.

questions.question: After organizing all help articles, which menu option is used to publish the Help Center to the web?

options.text: Export → PDF
options.explanation: Exporting to PDF creates a file, not a web‑published page.

options.text: Share → Publish tab
options.explanation: The guide instructs to publish via the Publish tab of the Share menu.

options.text: Settings → Workspace permissions
options.explanation: Workspace permissions control access, not publishing.

options.text: File → Save as HTML
options.explanation: Notion does not have a direct ‘Save as HTML’ option for publishing.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of subpages in the Help Center structure?

options.text: Subpages act as separate databases that cannot be linked to the main page.
options.explanation: Subpages can contain databases and can be linked; the statement is inaccurate.

options.text: Subpages organize individual help articles, allowing each to have its own icon and cover image.
options.explanation: The guide advises adding Help Center articles as subpages and customizing each with icons and cover images.

options.text: Subpages replace the need for a top‑level navigation menu.
options.explanation: Subpages supplement navigation but do not replace a navigation system.

options.text: Subpages are only used for internal team documentation, not public Help Centers.
options.explanation: The article demonstrates subpages as part of the public Help Center.

questions.question: If you want to ensure that updates to a frequently edited FAQ are reflected everywhere it appears, which workflow should you follow?

options.text: Copy‑paste the FAQ content into each page manually after each edit.
options.explanation: Manual copy‑pasting is error‑prone and defeats the purpose of sync.

options.text: Create a single page for the FAQ and use the Paste and sync feature to embed it in other pages.
options.explanation: The Paste and sync feature keeps content up‑to‑date across multiple locations.

options.text: Export the FAQ as a PDF and attach it to each page.
options.explanation: PDF attachments are static and won’t reflect future edits.

options.text: Use a third‑party integration to duplicate the FAQ content.
options.explanation: While possible, the guide does not mention third‑party duplication; the built‑in sync feature is preferred.

questions.question: Which resource is suggested for finding additional templates beyond the Help Center template?

options.text: The Notion mobile app’s template section.
options.explanation: The article does not reference the mobile app for template browsing.

options.text: Notion’s template gallery, accessible via the Templates section in the sidebar.
options.explanation: The guide points readers to browse over 10,000 templates in the template gallery.

options.text: A third‑party website that sells Notion templates.
options.explanation: External sites are not mentioned in the guide.

options.text: The Help Center’s own “Visit the help center” link.
options.explanation: That link leads to Notion’s help center, not a template repository.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to share the completed Help Center guide with others, according to the article?

options.text: Send them a PDF export of the Help Center page.
options.explanation: Exporting to PDF is not mentioned as the primary sharing method.

options.text: Publish the page to the web and provide the public URL.
options.explanation: The article explains that after setting up the Help Center, you publish it via the Share menu to obtain a public link.

options.text: Invite them as collaborators with edit permissions.
options.explanation: While possible, the guide focuses on publishing for public consumption rather than internal collaboration.

options.text: Embed the Help Center inside a Slack channel.
options.explanation: Embedding in Slack is not discussed in the article.
